violations.description: Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. -Raw meat on skewers stored above cooked chicken wings in 1 door line cooler.

General equipment - Design and construction. — --Food contact surface of equipment did not meet a design characteristic specified in the food service code. -Use scoops with handles in spice bins...not bowls.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -Clean spice bins and rice cookers.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -All shelving in kitchen needs cleaned. -Clean fan covers in walk in cooler.
Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitize solution for mechanical warewashing machine was not maintained at the proper concentration. -Jug was empty.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. -Single use trays etc. were stored on the floor. Store up off of floor.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…)were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -Entire kitchen needs cleaned.

General premises. — --Opening to outside or portion of building that is not part of the food establishment was not protected against the entry of insects and rodents. -Keep back doors sealed so flies etc. cant get in.
Control of insects, rodents, and other pests. — --Insect electrocution or trapping device was installed in an inappropriate location. -Multiple fly strips in food prep areas.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. -Multiple scoop handles found laying in spice bins.

Thawing potentially hazardous food. — --Potentially hazardous food was not thawed in an approved manner. -Tubs of chicken and beef in back area thawing. Must be thawed under cold running water or under refrigeration.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. -Remove cardboard and foil from shelving.

General equipment - Design and construction. — --Food contact surface of equipment did not meet a design characteristic specified in the food service code. -Scoops for spices etc. need to have handles. Do not use single use cups and bowls.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Food contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in good repair or sound condition. -Replace broken storage bin lids and any other plastic containers that are damaged.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -Clean all shelving in kitchen and in walk in cooler. -Clean fan covers in walk in cooler. -Clean doors and handles on all refrigeration units.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -Clean all floors walls and ceilings in entire kitchen and storage areas.
General premises. — --Perimeter walls and roof did not effectively protect establishment from the weather and entry of insects, rodents, and other animals. -Back doors are not tight fitting.

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. -Hang mops to dry when not in use. 1 mop in mop bucket.
